Software Review

Stack Construction Review (2026)

Digital takeoff and estimating software that replaces the blueprint printer and the highlighter. Here's the honest assessment.

⭐ Rating: 4.3/5 — Best dedicated digital takeoff tool for GCs and specialty contractors

Stack is a cloud-based construction estimating and takeoff platform. Its core value proposition is simple: upload your digital plan sets, measure quantities directly from the drawings (linear feet, square footage, volume, count), and build your estimates from those measured quantities. The result is faster, more accurate takeoffs with a documented, auditable measurement record.

For estimators who still print plans and use a scale ruler, Stack is a direct productivity upgrade. For those who've been using competing takeoff tools, it's worth understanding where Stack is stronger and where others have the edge.

Pricing

Plan Price Key Features
Starter ~$209/mo (annual) 1 user, basic takeoff, standard estimating
Pro ~$299/mo (annual) 3 users, all takeoff types, estimate templates, integrations
Enterprise Custom Unlimited users, advanced reporting, priority support

Pricing note: Stack pricing has been updated frequently — verify current rates directly with Stack. Annual billing saves approximately 20% vs. month-to-month. A free trial is available.

Core features

Digital takeoff

Stack's digital takeoff is its strongest feature. You upload plan PDFs (or import from Dropbox, Google Drive, or Box), and the measurement tools overlay directly on the drawings. Measurement types include:

  • Linear: Wall lengths, pipe runs, duct runs, conduit
  • Area: Floor area, wall area, roofing, paving, painting
  • Volume: Concrete pours, excavation, fill
  • Count: Doors, windows, fixtures, outlets

The measurement process is fast — calibrate the drawing scale once per sheet, then click and drag to measure. Stack auto-calculates quantities and the measurements are saved, so anyone reviewing the estimate can see exactly where each quantity came from.

Estimating database

Stack maintains an estimating database where you store your labor rates, material prices, and assembly costs. When you've taken off quantities, you apply your assemblies to get from raw quantities to complete estimates. You can import your own pricing, connect to supplier pricing (depending on integration availability in your region), or use Stack's built-in cost library as a starting point.

Estimate organization

Estimates in Stack are organized by work breakdown structure — sections, divisions, and line items that match your CSI format or custom structure. This makes it straightforward to generate subtotals by trade, scope, or building area. For GCs compiling bids from multiple subs, the organization tools help you build a complete bid package from disparate sub bids.

Reporting and proposal output

Stack generates professional estimate reports with summary and detailed views. You can create bid packages with scope sheets for sub solicitation, and export in formats that import into Excel or other tools. The client-facing proposal output is functional — not as polished as Buildertrend's proposal tools, but sufficient for professional bid submission.

Collaboration

Cloud-based access means multiple estimators can work on the same bid simultaneously. Drawing markup and measurements are shared in real time. For teams with multiple estimators or situations where a PM and estimator need to collaborate on a bid, cloud access is a genuine advantage over desktop-only tools.

What Stack does well

  • Fast, accurate digital takeoff with clear measurement documentation
  • Cloud access — no local installation, accessible from any device
  • Good drawing organization with version management
  • Solid estimating database with assembly-based costing
  • Free trial available — easy to evaluate before committing
  • Works for both GCs and specialty contractors (electrical, mechanical, civil)

Where Stack falls short

  • Not a PM platform — Stack handles estimating and takeoff only. You'll need a separate tool for project management, scheduling, and financials
  • Price — $209–$299/mo is real money for a single-purpose tool. Buildxact offers similar capabilities with more PM features at comparable price
  • Estimating database setup — The database is powerful once built out, but initial setup requires time investment to populate with your costs
  • Integration limitations — Integrates with some PM platforms but not all. Verify compatibility with your existing tools before purchasing
  • BIM/3D takeoff — For 3D model-based takeoff, specialized tools are more capable. Stack is primarily 2D plan-based

Who Stack is for

  • GCs and specialty contractors who estimate regularly and want to replace manual plan takeoff
  • Estimating departments with multiple people working on concurrent bids
  • Contractors who need documented, auditable quantity takeoff records
  • Companies already using a PM platform and just need a better estimating tool

Stack vs. the main alternatives

  • vs. Buildxact: Similar price, but Buildxact includes more project management features. Stack has a stronger pure-estimating focus. See our detailed comparison →
  • vs. PlanSwift: Desktop-based competitor; Stack's cloud approach is more flexible for remote teams
  • vs. Buildertrend (Complete plan): Buildertrend includes estimating but it's not the main focus. Stack is deeper for dedicated estimating operations

Frequently asked questions

Does Stack work for residential construction?

Yes. Stack is used across residential, commercial, and specialty contracting. The takeoff tools work the same regardless of project type. That said, for residential builders who want estimating integrated with project management, client portals, and scheduling, Buildertrend or Buildxact may be a more complete solution.

Can Stack import from PDF plans?

Yes — Stack works primarily with PDF plan sets. You upload PDFs, set the scale, and measure directly from the drawing. It also integrates with Dropbox, Google Drive, and Box for plan management.

Is there a free version of Stack?

Stack offers a free trial but not a permanent free tier. The trial gives you enough time to evaluate whether the takeoff workflow fits your process before committing to a paid plan.

Affiliate disclosure: Links to Buildertrend and Houzz Pro are affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you sign up through our link. This review is based on independent research. Full disclosure →
